name: Homemade Sauerkraut
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the cabbage thoroughly and trim away any bruised or damaged leaves.
2. Quarter and cut away the core, then discard it. Cut the cabbage into uniformly fine shreds using a food processor, mandoline, or knife.
3. In a large plastic tub, mix half of the shredded cabbage with half of the salt. Let stand for 5-10 minutes to allow juices to begin coming out of the cabbage.
4. Place this mixture in the fermenting tub. Repeat with the remaining cabbage and salt, packing it tightly into the tub.
5. Ensure the cabbage is completely covered by juice to prevent spoilage. If necessary, make additional brine by mixing 4 tablespoons salt with 2 quarts water and pouring over the top.
6. Securely cover the tub with plastic wrap, then a clean towel to keep out contaminants.
7. Store in a room temperature of 65-75°F and do not uncover for at least 3 weeks.
8. Check the jar periodically by gently tapping it; there should be no bubbles of CO2 gas evident in the brine. If there are, recover and check again in 2-3 days.
9. The finished sauerkraut should have a clean appearance with no white spots or unpleasant odors. Texture should be firm.
10. Pack the finished sauerkraut into clean jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
